name: Can I sue my boss if they knew about the toxic fumes causing my cancer but did nothing?
description: Unlikely due to ODA exclusivity; intentional tort exception unextended to diseases.[1]

mainEntity.name: Can I sue my boss if they knew about the toxic fumes causing my cancer but did nothing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Unlikely due to ODA exclusivity; intentional tort exception unextended to diseases.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Probably not, as Utah's ODA provides exclusive remedies without a clear intentional tort exception for occupational diseases yet.[1] The Utah Supreme Court remanded a lead poisoning case to check if intentional tort applies to ODA claims, hinting it might not.[1] Key factors: prove employer knew of certain harm and acted intentionally, distinguish ODA from WCA, and overcome exclusivity barriers.[1] Document everything including complaints ignored, but expect workers' comp as primary path; rare suits require strong evidence. Consult for case-specific viability.
